title: cinnamomum longepaniculatum
intro: Cinnamomum longepaniculatum, also known as the Fragrant Evergreen or Indonesian Camphor, is a plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae. It is a large evergreen tree native to Indonesia, with aromatic leaves and bark used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves and bark of Cinnamomum longepaniculatum are rich in camphor, which has anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves can be prepared as a herbal tea, and its essential oil can be extracted for use in aromatherapy, providing various health benefits, including stress relief and improved respiratory health.
